title: juncus bufonius subsp. foliosus
intro: Juncus bufonius subsp. foliosus, also known as the 'Foliolate Marsh Rush' or 'Foliolate Toad Rush', is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. The health benefits of Juncus bufonius subsp. foliosus include its potential to aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or using them in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
